notes:
Arrival

Reception is on Level 2. Ask for the charge nurse on arrival, badge collection takes about 5 minutes.

Parking validation available at the front desk if you drove in.

Emergency Bay 4

Resus bay during the late shift. Fast-paced, expect 4 to 6 active presentations through your shift.

Required:

  • Advanced Life Support current
  • ED experience within last 12 months
  • Comfortable running primary assessment independently

Senior consultant on the floor 1500 to 2300.

What you'll cover

  • 4-bed acute observation bay
  • Medication rounds at 0800, 1200, and 1700
  • End-of-shift handover to incoming RN
Handover at start of shift, arrive 10 min early.

Mental Health Adult Inpatient

16-bed adult acute mental health unit. Mix of voluntary and involuntary admissions under the NSW Mental Health Act.

Required

  • Mental Health First Aid certificate
  • De-escalation training within 24 months
  • Recent inpatient mental health experience

Two staff at all times in the seclusion review process. You will not be the sole RN on shift.

Mental Health Adult Inpatient

16-bed adult acute mental health unit. Mix of voluntary and involuntary admissions under the NSW Mental Health Act.

Required

  • Mental Health First Aid certificate
  • De-escalation training within 24 months
  • Recent inpatient mental health experience

Two staff at all times in the seclusion review process. You will not be the sole RN on shift.

Arrival

Reception is on Level 2. Ask for the charge nurse on arrival, badge collection takes about 5 minutes.

Parking validation available at the front desk if you drove in.

Heads-up

This is a night shift covering the post-anaesthesia care unit. Lighter caseload than days but you're the senior RN on the floor.

  • 1:4 ratio overnight
  • Nurse-in-charge takes the bleep
  • Fresh post-op patients arrive from theatre until ~2200

Heads-up

This is a night shift covering the post-anaesthesia care unit. Lighter caseload than days but you're the senior RN on the floor.

  • 1:4 ratio overnight
  • Nurse-in-charge takes the bleep
  • Fresh post-op patients arrive from theatre until ~2200
Familiar with PRN medication administration preferred.

Maternity, Birthing Suite

Active labour ward, two birthing rooms. You'll be paired with the senior midwife on shift.

Bring

  • Stethoscope
  • Pinard or doppler if you have a preferred unit
  • Closed-toe shoes (theatre may be needed)

Lactation consultant on call from 0800.

Required

  • AHPRA registration current
  • Manual handling within 12 months
  • Mental Health First Aid certificate

Preferred: recent palliative care experience.
Please bring your own scrubs and stethoscope.

Emergency Bay 4

Resus bay during the late shift. Fast-paced, expect 4 to 6 active presentations through your shift.

Required:

  • Advanced Life Support current
  • ED experience within last 12 months
  • Comfortable running primary assessment independently

Senior consultant on the floor 1500 to 2300.
Bondi parking is limited, public transport recommended. Bus 333 stops directly outside.

Heads-up

Patient cohort includes one PRN sedation order. Comfort administering it is essential.

One patient is on contact precautions, PPE station is at the bay entrance.

Arrival

Reception is on Level 2. Ask for the charge nurse on arrival, badge collection takes about 5 minutes.

Parking validation available at the front desk if you drove in.

Aged Care, Memory Support

Dedicated dementia wing, 12 residents. Established care plans for each, focus is on continuity and gentle redirection.

  • Medication round at 0800 (Webster packs)
  • Mealtime support 0830, 1230, 1730
  • Sundowning management 1600 onwards

Familiarity with the Bondi Memory Support model is a plus but not required, the team will walk you through it.
Handover at start of shift, arrive 10 min early.

Heads-up

Patient cohort includes one PRN sedation order. Comfort administering it is essential.

One patient is on contact precautions, PPE station is at the bay entrance.
Step-down from ICU patient cohort. Recent acute experience preferred.

Aged Care, Memory Support

Dedicated dementia wing, 12 residents. Established care plans for each, focus is on continuity and gentle redirection.

  • Medication round at 0800 (Webster packs)
  • Mealtime support 0830, 1230, 1730
  • Sundowning management 1600 onwards

Familiarity with the Bondi Memory Support model is a plus but not required, the team will walk you through it.

About this shift

Acute medical ward, 6-bed bay. Most patients are post-surgical day 2 to 3, ambulating with assistance.

You'll be responsible for

  • Hourly observations on bay 3
  • Wound care (one PICC line, one surgical drain)
  • Medication administration including IV antibiotics
  • Discharge planning for two patients due to leave Friday

Handover: 0700 sharp at the nurses' station.

Arrival

Reception is on Level 2. Ask for the charge nurse on arrival, badge collection takes about 5 minutes.

Parking validation available at the front desk if you drove in.

Palliative Care wing

8 patients, all in the active phase of palliative care. The pace is slower than acute, the emotional load is higher.

What we value: gentleness, patience, comfort with grief.

  • Symptom management (pain, nausea, dyspnoea)
  • Family support and presence
  • End-of-life care including verification of death

If this is your first palliative shift with us, the charge nurse will spend the first 30 min orientating you.
Familiar with PRN medication administration preferred.
Step-down from ICU patient cohort. Recent acute experience preferred.

Maternity, Birthing Suite

Active labour ward, two birthing rooms. You'll be paired with the senior midwife on shift.

Bring

  • Stethoscope
  • Pinard or doppler if you have a preferred unit
  • Closed-toe shoes (theatre may be needed)

Lactation consultant on call from 0800.
Step-down from ICU patient cohort. Recent acute experience preferred.

Outpatients clinic

Pre-admission clinic, mostly elective surgical patients. Day shift, predictable pace.

  • Vital signs and basic obs on each patient
  • Medication reconciliation
  • Pre-op education and consent witnessing

Standard business attire under scrubs preferred, this is a public-facing clinic.

Aged Care, Memory Support

Dedicated dementia wing, 12 residents. Established care plans for each, focus is on continuity and gentle redirection.

  • Medication round at 0800 (Webster packs)
  • Mealtime support 0830, 1230, 1730
  • Sundowning management 1600 onwards

Familiarity with the Bondi Memory Support model is a plus but not required, the team will walk you through it.
Familiar with PRN medication administration preferred.
Step-down from ICU patient cohort. Recent acute experience preferred.
